title: Campaign with the Big Issue - Big Issue
meta_description: Campaign with the Big Issue and join thousands of like minded people that want to end poverty in the UK once and for all.

h3: Our movement to end poverty
h3: Unlock exclusive access to the Big Issue
text_excerpt: Campaign with the Big Issue - Big Issue Skip to header Skip to main content Skip to footer Subscribe £ Support PETITION Menu Big Issue Group Big Issue Group Our Group Impact Changing Lives C.I.C Big Issue Invest Big Issue Recruit Impact Advisory Big Issue Shop Big Issue Trust Our Ambassadors Partner with us News News Housing Employment Health Environment Politics Advice Advice Living Costs Debt and Benefits Jobs Culture Culture Books Film Music TV Letter To My Younger Self Big Issue Magazine Big Issue Magazine Magazines Find your local vendor Vendor Support Service Become a Vendor Vendor stories Advertise with us Support Us Support Us Ways to support Transformation Fund Leave a Legacy Campaign with us Volunteer Search Search Search Special offer: Enjoy 8 weeks of Big Issue for just £9.99, delivered weekly. SUBSCRIBE Home Campaign with us Campaign with us Share Become a Big Issue campaign supporter. Take action today and join a movement to create a better future for tomorrow. Campaign with Big Issue We’re building a movement to end poverty in the UK KEEP ME UPDATED Big Issue is campaigning to end poverty in the UK Right now, over 6.8 million people in the UK are experiencing very deep poverty . That’s 6.8 million people struggling to afford to meet their most basic needs to stay warm, dry, clean and fed. We need politicians to commit to investing in people to end poverty and improve society for everyone. Our latest campaign Poverty Zero is fighting for the change we need. Active campaigns Poverty Zero Poverty Zero READ MORE End No-Fault Evictions End No-Fault Evictions READ MORE Blueprint to End Poverty Blueprint to End Poverty READ MORE Our movement to end poverty Campaigning with the Big Issue has never been more exciting. We have a network of thousands of digital activists, journalists, and ambassadors speaking up for change. This network is supported in government in the House of Lords by our founder Lord Bird . We’re raising the issues that matter most when it comes to tackling poverty and holding politicians to account. We need your all to speak up now. It doesn’t matter who you are, we need your help. Jane, Big Issue vendor and campaigner Our poverty prevention pillars We believe that ending poverty relies upon addressing five interlinked pillars of concern. Poverty affects people in many different ways, and there is no one-size-fits-all solution. These pillars are the cornerstone of our campaigning and broader work at the Big Issue Group, and we believe that the government must adopt the same model to truly break the cycle of poverty. Housing : secure housing is an important step in creating a stable environment to move forward and rebuild peoples’ lives. Health & Wellbeing : poverty causes ill health, which drives inequality and increases the pressure on health services. Prevention improves quality of life, and is more cost-effective for the state. Community & Environment : communities can directly tackle poverty and inequality through local initiatives. These in turn will improve our environment. Employment & Learning : investing in lifelong education and skills results in more stable and secure employment, higher earnings and less exposure to poverty. Financial & Digital Inclusion : we tackle digital and financial exclusion by ensuring everyone has basic financial and digital skills, as well as access to the internet, communication tools, and bank accounts. This is critical for driving inclusion and enabling earning and employment. We have also used our poverty prevention pillars to structure our Blueprint to End Poverty . These are the short, medium, and long-term policy areas that we believe the government needs to address to end poverty in the UK for good. Our work in government Learn more about how our work is supported in government in the House of Lords by Big Issue founder founder Lord Bird. Taking the fight to end poverty into Parliament. LEARN MORE We’re working to embed poverty prevention in the heart of go
